authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 20000-1:2018 Information technology - Service management
authoritative_sources.publisher: International Organization for Standardization / International Electrotechnical Commission
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO/IEC 20000-1 is an international service-management system standard and is authoritative for service request, incident, change, service-level, supplier, and continual-improvement discipline in IT and hosted-service delivery.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service intake, request classification, service-level context, and resolution discipline

authoritative_sources.title: ITIL 4 Practice: Service Request Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ITIL is a recognized public IT service-management framework; the service request practice directly informs supported request handling, communication, and escalation boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Supported request handling and stakeholder communication

authoritative_sources.title: TMF621 Trouble Ticket Management API
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TM Forum Open APIs are recognized telecom and digital-service interface standards; the official public tmforum-apis mirror for TMF621 establishes trouble-ticket records, status, severity, related entities, and handoff patterns used in connectivity incident workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: TMF641 Service Ordering Management API
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TMF641 is a recognized public service-order specification for telecom and digital service delivery; the official public tmforum-apis mirror grounds order state, requested service changes, dependencies, and delivery documentation.

authoritative_sources.title: MEF 48.1 Service Activation Testing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: MEF is a recognized standards body for carrier Ethernet and connectivity services; service activation testing is directly authoritative for turn-up and acceptance evidence in connectivity service delivery.
used_for: completion refusal when test evidence is missing

authoritative_sources.title: DC OIX-2 Certification Standards and Certification for Data Centers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Open-IX develops open standards for internet infrastructure and OIX-2 addresses data-center certification expectations for interconnection environments, making it directly relevant to cross-connect and meet-me-room support terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Interconnection terminology and facility handoff context

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-61 Rev. 3, Incident Response Recommendations and Considerations for Cybersecurity Risk Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3 is an official federal cybersecurity incident-response publication and constrains information sharing, detection, response, recovery, and communication when connectivity service delivery intersects incident response.
used_for: incident response and recovery communication constraints

authoritative_sources.title: Trust Services Criteria for Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The AICPA Trust Services Criteria are recognized service-organization control criteria for availability, confidentiality, processing integrity, privacy, and security commitments that inform hosted-service quality-review and customer commitment boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: 47 CFR Part 4 - Disruptions to Communications
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations / Federal Communications Commission
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 47 CFR Part 4 is primary U.S. communications outage-reporting regulation. It defines reportable outage thresholds, special-facility notification content, and authorized submission requirements; the pack uses it to escalate regulatory-reporting questions instead of letting service delivery decide them.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Outage, regulatory-reporting, and authorized-signoff boundary handling

workflow_stages.name: Service support intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm customer, tenant, product, environment, facility, service identifiers, request type, customer impact, communication constraints, and authority map before deciding whether the request is in-scope, blocked, refused, or escalated.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Authoritative record and dependency retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Retrieve and reconcile ticket, CRM, service-order, CMDB, service inventory, NMS, DCIM, carrier, project-management, document repository, access-control, telemetry, test, and communication-log evidence before stating service status.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Supported request resolution and customer communication
workflow_stages.description: Resolve supported connectivity service questions using documented service state and approved customer wording while avoiding engineering, security, legal, editorial, regulatory, financial, and contractual conclusions.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Connectivity and hosted-service evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Check circuit, cross-connect, LOA/CFA, port, VLAN, route, BGP, carrier handoff, cloud on-ramp, CDN origin, NMS alarm, activation-test, maintenance-window, and customer-confirmation artifacts before closure.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Confidentiality, approval, and commitment screening
workflow_stages.description: Check recipient authorization, channel approval, tenant confidentiality rules, service commitments, quality-review gates, and customer notification constraints before sending service records or incident updates.

workflow_stages.name: Exception, repeat-failure, and boundary escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package unsupported workarounds, repeat failures, missing evidence, customer complaints, source conflicts, outage-reporting questions, and authority-sensitive asks for the correct adjacent owner.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: Customer request, service ticket, trouble ticket, incident record, CRM case, contact authorization, and communication thread
Service order, project plan, maintenance window, change reference, delivery milestone, approval record, and customer commitment

Connectivity inventory, CMDB asset, circuit ID, cross-connect order, LOA/CFA, demarc, patch-panel map, port, VLAN, VRF, ASN, BGP session, route, IP prefix, cloud on-ramp, CDN origin, and DNS dependency
NMS alarms, interface counters, optical levels, telemetry snapshots, ping or traceroute results, route table summaries, activation-test outputs, and customer-confirmation evidence
DCIM location, rack, power dependency, MMR, facility alert, access-control authorization, remote-hands note, and carrier portal record
Root cause summary, incident bridge log, outage notification draft, regulatory escalation package, service operations summary, QA review note, and resolution note
Confidentiality constraints, recipient approval list, legal or editorial review record, service-credit or account escalation, and contract-boundary evidence
systems_of_record: Service inventory, CMDB, and asset registry
Network monitoring, observability, and telemetry platform
DCIM, facility operations, and access-control systems
Document management, content workflow, and communication-log repository
Quality-review, incident-management, regulatory escalation, and approval-routing systems

allowed: Resolve supported connectivity service requests when tenant service model, customer authorization, service identifiers, systems of record, and evidence support the response.
Request minimum missing facts, system records, approvals, and customer or tenant context needed to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, customer or stakeholder communication summaries, service operations summaries, and escalation packets without overstating authority.

Route the case to the correct adjacent owner when the workflow becomes network engineering, security, site operations, legal, editorial, regulatory, account, carrier, quality-review, delivery-management, or customer-approval work.
escalate: Required tenant facts, service identifiers, customer commitments, facility context, approval rules, or source records are missing or contradictory.

The request seeks a policy exception, unsupported workaround, access exception, security exception, service-credit decision, outage classification, or customer communication outside documented authority.
Professional judgment, client or subscriber rights, content rights, security posture, regulatory reporting, legal interpretation, editorial wording, or contractual signoff determines the answer.
Public standards, tenant policy, and customer contract materially disagree on what can be communicated, delivered, or represented.
refuse: Requests for engineering, security, legal, editorial, regulatory, financial, service-credit, or contractual approval without explicit authorized owner.
Requests to fabricate approvals, claim service restoration without evidence, disclose confidential records to unapproved parties, or assert unsupported outage or SLA status.
Requests to bypass documented change, access, confidentiality, quality-review, retention, service-level, pricing, or approval controls.

domain_failure_modes: Telling a customer connectivity is restored when NMS alarms, carrier status, service activation tests, and customer confirmation are missing or contradictory.
Treating service delivery as authority to modify BGP policy, firewall rules, DDoS controls, routing design, or physical access controls.
Disclosing topology, traffic, incident, vulnerability, credential, customer, or content-delivery records to an unapproved recipient or channel.
Using generic customer-service language that hides circuit, cross-connect, port, service-order, activation-test, maintenance-window, or carrier-handoff evidence.
Publishing outage, SLA breach, service-credit, public incident, legal, editorial, or contractual conclusions instead of handing off with evidence.
Closing repeat packet-loss, cross-connect, or carrier-handoff failures without preserving ticket history, owner routing, and corrective-action expectations.
Ignoring tenant-specific QA review, customer approver, and confidentiality controls because public service-management guidance is more general.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific precedence among ticketing, CRM, service inventory, carrier portal, DCIM, NMS, and customer confirmation records
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Treat missing source precedence as escalation-only; do not claim resolution until tenant precedence or owner review is retrieved.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific communications outage, privacy, lawful access, or customer notification obligations may be stricter than the public baseline used here
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require tenant jurisdiction, product, regulatory owner, and contract context at runtime; route regulated interpretation to authorized legal or regulatory owner.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Customer contract may define bespoke SLA, service-credit, notification, and premium-event communication rules
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require contract and account owner retrieval before customer-facing commitment language; escalate service-credit or contract-classification questions.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated release because tenant-specific policy, jurisdiction, and contract questions are converted into runtime prerequisites and escalation rules.
Source classes cover workflow, evidence, terminology, confidentiality, quality-review, and failure handling.
